name: Niche Rejuvenation and Support Coverage Assessment for June 12, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Backfill assessment of the published June 2 niche-rejuvenation baseline and the June 5 no-op surveillance session; no new external source search was run for this assessment.

summary: Coverage is adequate for the current low-confidence animal_signal public claim. The local map includes systemic-milieu and plasma-dilution aged-mouse anchors, direct aged-muscle niche mechanisms through oxytocin, fibronectin reconstitution, and myofiber NF-kB inhibition, and an HSC boundary showing that bloodborne rejuvenation is not tissue-general. Coverage is not strong because direct human niche-engagement evidence is absent, safety and durability are unresolved for chronic or systemic niche manipulation, recent-review and formal seminal-anchor triangulation are incomplete, and the track still sits near circulating-factor, neuroendocrine, stem-cell rejuvenation, and tissue-repair boundaries. The June 5 surveillance pass found no material post-publication delta, so ordinary surveillance can continue.

evidence_categories.rationale: The assessed artifacts do not include a result-bearing targeted human niche-support intervention. Human therapeutic plasma exchange appears only as adjacent context in the plasma-dilution source and was explicitly not used to upgrade the track beyond animal_signal; the June 5 surveillance pass also found no targeted human niche-engagement result.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A human upgrade would require in vivo evidence that a niche-support intervention moves aging-relevant niche markers and improves tissue function.

evidence_categories.rationale: The bootstrap and no-op surveillance artifacts did not systematically map human observational, biopsy, natural-experiment, plasma-exchange, sarcopenia, or tissue-repair cohorts for stem-cell niche aging.
evidence_categories.gap_note: This is not blocking for the current animal_signal claim, but future repair could decide whether observational human niche-aging context would add useful limits.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline captures the current animal case: young systemic environment, neutral plasma dilution, oxytocin, fibronectin reconstitution, and myofiber NF-kB inhibition improve aged mouse progenitor, tissue, or muscle-repair readouts, while HSC refractoriness limits tissue-general claims.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The animal map is tissue- and injury-model heavy and does not include a lifespan anchor for this track.

evidence_categories.rationale: Mechanistic coverage spans systemic milieu, dilution of old plasma factors, endocrine support through oxytocin, extracellular-matrix niche restoration through fibronectin, cell-non-autonomous inflammatory signaling through myofiber NF-kB, and tissue-specific HSC resistance.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The mechanisms are not one unified intervention class, so future updates should keep systemic-factor removal, endocrine support, ECM restoration, and inflammatory niche modulation separate.

evidence_categories.rationale: The evidence map includes aged-HSC refractoriness as direct limiting evidence and preserves the human plasma-exchange boundary: adjacent human serum or biomarker context is not treated as direct human stem-cell niche rejuvenation evidence.
evidence_categories.gap_note: More limiting evidence could help separate positive aged-muscle repair signals from tissue-general rejuvenation claims.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public outlook flags risks from systemic factor removal, endocrine signaling, inflammatory suppression, and matrix remodeling, but the assessed source map does not contain dedicated long-term safety or durability records for chronic niche manipulation.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Safety and durability should remain high-priority evidence requirements before confidence or human relevance is strengthened.

evidence_categories.rationale: The June 5 surveillance session checked ClinicalTrials.gov for adjacent aging plasma-exchange and stem-cell or satellite-cell niche terms. Adjacent plasma-exchange records remained no-results context, and no targeted human niche-support intervention with niche-engagement or functional result data was found.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Registry status should be rechecked during ordinary surveillance, especially for targeted niche-support, plasma-exchange, muscle-regeneration, sarcopenia, or stem-cell niche terms.

evidence_categories.rationale: The backfill reviewed local bootstrap and surveillance artifacts. A broad pre-window review was noted as context-only during surveillance, but recent review and meta-analysis triangulation has not been formally reconciled against the included source map.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A later coverage repair pass could compare recent stem-cell niche, muscle regeneration, systemic milieu, and plasma-exchange reviews against the current anchors.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline includes plausible anchor papers for heterochronic parabiosis, plasma dilution, oxytocin, fibronectin niche reconstitution, myofiber NF-kB signaling, and HSC refractoriness. It is not strong because no formal must-include or citation-network audit is recorded.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Formal review triangulation or citation chasing could move this category from adequate to strong.

evidence_categories.rationale: The bootstrap notes and evidence reviews preserve boundaries with stem-cell rejuvenation, circulating-factor modulation, therapeutic plasma exchange, cell-intrinsic HSC rejuvenation, and broader tissue repair. The June 5 surveillance pass also excluded adjacent 15-PGDH muscle-repair and skin-fibroblast ECM records as not material to this track.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The boundary remains important because systemic milieu, endocrine, ECM, and inflammatory-niche evidence can overlap neighboring tracks.

search_log_summary: This backfill reviewed local project artifacts: the published Niche Rejuvenation and Support track outlook, June 2 bootstrap session, published candidate bundle, source-fidelity and interpretation reviews, publication event, and June 5 no-op surveillance session with its PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, preprint, and web-search log. No new PubMed, registry, preprint, citation-network, or web source search was run for this assessment.
source_selection_notes: PMID 15716955 and PMID 32474458 anchor systemic-milieu and plasma-dilution support while preserving the clinical-translatability boundary.
PMID 24915299, PMID 27376579, and PMID 27852976 anchor direct aged-muscle niche mechanisms through oxytocin, fibronectin, and myofiber NF-kB signaling.
PMID 34032859 is included as boundary evidence because aged HSCs did not regain youthful function after bloodborne systemic rejuvenation interventions.
Human therapeutic plasma exchange and aging registry context is treated as adjacent unless a study measures stem-cell niche engagement and functional tissue repair.
The June 5 surveillance pass found no material post-publication source, no correction or retraction for the six supporting PMIDs, and no relevant posted trial result.

known_gaps.description: No result-bearing human intervention shows aging-relevant stem-cell niche marker movement linked to tissue repair, strength, hematopoietic, neurogenic, or resilience outcomes.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep the public stage at animal_signal until a targeted human niche-support intervention reports niche engagement and functional or disease-relevant outcomes.

known_gaps.description: The source map does not establish long-term safety, durability, dosing boundaries, fibrosis, tumor, immune, endocrine, or repair tradeoffs for systemic or local niche manipulation.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Require explicit safety and durability review before strengthening confidence, momentum, or human relevance for any niche-support intervention.

known_gaps.description: Recent reviews and meta-analyses around stem-cell niches, systemic milieu, plasma dilution, aged muscle repair, and niche support have not been reconciled against the current source list.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Use a future coverage-repair pass or surveillance review to compare recent reviews with the six-anchor source map and document any missing decision-relevant records.

known_gaps.description: The baseline includes plausible anchor papers, but no formal must-include, review-cited, or citation-network audit has been recorded for the track.
known_gaps.suggested_action: During a future source-completeness pass, audit highly cited and review-cited niche-rejuvenation anchors against the current source list.

known_gaps.description: Niche rejuvenation overlaps circulating-factor modulation, neuroendocrine signaling, stem-cell rejuvenation, inflammatory modulation, and tissue repair, so future source additions can easily blur track scope.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep future staged records explicit about whether they test systemic milieu, local niche support, cell-intrinsic stem-cell rejuvenation, endocrine signaling, inflammatory repair, or general tissue repair.

next_coverage_action: Continue ordinary surveillance for Niche Rejuvenation and Support. On the next pass, recheck targeted niche-support, plasma-exchange, muscle-regeneration, sarcopenia, and stem-cell niche terms for human niche-engagement results or meaningful registry movement; reserve coverage_repair for a later review-triangulation or formal seminal-anchor audit if no material field delta appears.
